Avetta PQF - Australia Offices and Locations of Work Performed

If you have workers performing work for an extended period, on a permanent basis, or if the state/territory selected is your main base of operation, you must provide state/territory-specific Workers Compensation documentation.\nIf you have workers performing occasional, short-term work for a selected state/territory, you may be required to provide state/territory-specific Workers Compensation documentation.
Does your company have a main base of operation or perform work in any of the following locations (regardless of whether there is a physical office)?

Based on your selected responses, it appears that workers' compensation coverage may not apply to your business in Australia. \nPlease take a moment to review your answers and ensure you've chosen the state or territory that reflects your principal place of business. This is defined as the main base of operation where your business activities are primarily managed or conducted in Australia—usually the address linked to your Australian Business Number (ABN). \nIf workers' compensation coverage is not applicable to your business, please select one of the options below to indicate the reason:
We are an overseas supplier and do not have personnel attending site in person
We provide offsite support or professional services and do not have personnel attending site in person
We are a manufacturer or goods supplier and do not have personnel attending site in person
Other (please specify)
